paper.title: Differential Transformer
paper.abstract: Transformer tends to overallocate attention to irrelevant context. In this work, we introduce Diff Transformer, which amplifies attention to the relevant context while canceling noise. Specifically, the differential attention mechanism calculates attention scores as the difference between two separate softmax attention maps. The subtraction cancels noise, promoting the emergence of sparse attention patterns. Experimental results on language modeling show that Diff Transformer outperforms Transformer in various settings of scaling up model size and training tokens. More intriguingly, it offers notable advantages in practical applications, such as long-context modeling, key information retrieval, hallucination mitigation, in-context learning, and reduction of activation outliers. By being less distracted by irrelevant context, Diff Transformer can mitigate hallucination in question answering and text summarization. For in-context learning, Diff Transformer not only enhances accuracy but is also more robust to order permutation, which was considered as a chronic robustness issue. The results position Diff Transformer as a highly effective and promising architecture to advance large language models.

components.name: Input Embeddings
components.description: Pack input token embeddings into matrix X^0 = [x1, ..., xN] ∈ R^(N×d_model)

components.name: Rotary Position Embedding
components.description: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) used for positional encoding

hyperparameters.theta: RoPE base frequency, set to 640000 for 64K context

components.name: Query/Key Split Projection
components.description: Project input X to two sets of query and key vectors by splitting the output channel dimension
equations: [Q_1 ; Q_2] = X W^Q ,\quad [K_1 ; K_2] = X W^K

components.name: Value Projection
components.description: Project input X to value vectors with doubled output dimension

components.name: Differential Scalar λ
components.description: Learnable scalar that controls the differential attention weighting, re-parameterized from learnable vectors

components.name: Differential Attention (DiffAttn)
components.description: Core differential attention mechanism that computes attention scores as the difference between two softmax attention maps to cancel noise

components.name: Head-wise RMSNorm
components.description: RMSNorm applied to each attention head independently before concatenation

components.name: GroupNorm Scale Multiplier
components.description: Fixed multiplier (1 - λ_init) applied after GroupNorm to align gradient flow with Transformer

components.name: Multi-Head Concatenation
components.description: Concatenate outputs from all attention heads along channel dimension

components.name: Output Projection
components.description: Final linear projection to map concatenated head outputs back to d_model dimension

components.name: Multi-Head Differential Attention
components.description: Multi-head differential attention with GroupNorm normalization and fixed scale multiplier

components.name: Attention Residual Connection
components.description: Add differential attention output to input via residual connection

components.name: Pre-RMSNorm for Attention
components.description: RMSNorm applied to input before differential attention module

components.name: SwiGLU Feed-Forward Network
components.description: SwiGLU activation function with gated mechanism: SwiGLU(X) = (swish(X W^G) ⊙ X W_1) W_2

hyperparameters.W_2: second FFN projection ∈ R^(8/3 d_model × d_model)

components.name: FFN Residual Connection
components.description: Add SwiGLU FFN output to input via residual connection

components.name: Pre-RMSNorm for FFN
components.description: RMSNorm applied to attention output before SwiGLU FFN

components.name: DIFF Transformer Decoder Layer
components.description: Single DIFF Transformer layer consisting of multi-head differential attention followed by SwiGLU FFN with pre-RMSNorm

quote.text: Given an input sequence x = x1 · · · xN, we pack the input embeddings into X0 = [x1, · · · , xN] ∈RN×dmodel, where dmodel represents the hidden dimension of the model.

invariants.description: DIFF Transformer uses half the number of attention heads compared to standard Transformer to align parameter counts and computational complexity

quote.text: We set the number of heads h = dmodel/2d, where d is equal to the head dimension of Transformer. So we can align the parameter counts and computational complexity.

invariants.description: Pre-RMSNorm (not pre-LayerNorm) is used before attention and FFN modules following LLaMA design

quote.text: We also adopt pre-RMSNorm (Zhang & Sennrich, 2019) and SwiGLU (Shazeer, 2020; Ramachandran et al., 2017) as improvements following LLaMA (Touvron et al., 2023).

invariants.description: SwiGLU activation function is used in the FFN instead of ReLU or GELU

quote.text: SwiGLU(X) = (swish(XW G) ⊙XW1)W2, and W G, W1 ∈Rdmodel× 8/3 dmodel, W2 ∈R 8/3 dmodel×dmodel are learnable matrices.

invariants.description: λ_init is initialized with exponential decay across layers: λ_init = 0.8 - 0.6 × exp(-0.3 × (l-1))

quote.text: We empirically find that the setting λinit = 0.8 −0.6 × exp(−0.3 · (l −1)) works well in practice, where l ∈[1, L] represents layer index.

invariants.description: Fixed multiplier (1 - λ_init) is applied after GroupNorm to align gradient flow with Transformer

quote.text: We use a fixed multiplier (1−λinit) as the scale of LN(·) to align the gradients with Transformer.

invariants.description: LN (RMSNorm) is applied to each head independently before concatenation (GroupNorm semantics)

quote.text: LN(·) uses RMSNorm (Zhang & Sennrich, 2019) for each head, and Concat(·) concatenates the heads together along the channel dimension.

invariants.description: Bias is removed from all linear projections following LLaMA design

quote.text: The 'Transformer' models include improvements in RMSNorm (Zhang & Sennrich, 2019), SwiGLU (Shazeer, 2020; Ramachandran et al., 2017), and removal of bias.

symbol_table.d: head dimension (half of standard to align params)
symbol_table.W^Q, W^K, W^V: projection matrices for query, key, value
symbol_table.Q_1, Q_2: first and second group of query vectors
symbol_table.K_1, K_2: first and second group of key vectors
symbol_table.A_1, A_2: softmax attention maps for Q1/K1 and Q2/K2

notes: DIFF Transformer replaces standard softmax attention with differential attention that computes the difference between two softmax attention maps. This cancels attention noise and promotes sparse attention patterns. Key architectural differences: (1) half the number of heads to maintain parameter parity, (2) doubled Q/K projection output dimension split into two groups, (3) λ is a learnable scalar re-parameterized from vectors, (4) GroupNorm applied per-head with fixed (1-λ_init) scale, (5) pre-RMSNorm and SwiGLU following LLaMA. The differential attention mechanism is compatible with FlashAttention.
